goal: Prototype vision-guided bin picking in Isaac Sim: SICK PLB 3D camera + 300 settled transmission castings, pose estimator via FoundationPose / Isaac Manipulator, realistic cycle-time expectation.
thoughts: Don't tell Kenji to train a CNN from scratch — point at FoundationPose / DeepObjectPose / Isaac Manipulator as existing pose-estimation building blocks. Pile-of-parts via physics drop + settle is the real Isaac Sim workflow (not hand-authored pile). Realistic cycle time 3-8 s/pick for 6-DOF vision-guided bin pick. Honest hard parts: specular casting surfaces under factory lighting, bin-bottom occlusion, oil-coated BRDF calibration. SICK PLB mount pose + intrinsics from the datasheet.
code:
# 1. Pull SICK PLB calibration parameters (intrinsics, range, working distance)
lookup_product_spec(query="SICK PLB 3D camera intrinsics working range calibration mount position")

# 2. Existing Isaac Sim pose-estimation building blocks
lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Manipulator FoundationPose DeepObjectPose sim-to-real bin picking")

# 3. Place the 3D camera at the real-world mount pose (depth + RGB)
configure_camera(
    prim_path="/World/Cell/SICK_PLB",
    camera_type="standard",
    width="<W>", height="<H>",
    data_types=["rgb", "depth"],
    focal_length="<F>",
    offset_pos=("<MX>","<MY>","<MZ>"),
    offset_rot=("<QW>","<QX>","<QY>","<QZ>")
)

# 4. Scatter + physics-settle 300 castings in the bin (real workflow)
scatter_on_surface(
    asset_path="<CASTING_USD>",
    target_surface="/World/Cell/Bin",
    count=300,
    drop_height="<DROP_H>",
    spacing_min="<S_MIN>"
)
run_usd_script(script="""
# Let physics settle for N steps, then freeze poses for deterministic eval
for _ in range(<N_SETTLE_STEPS>):
    env.sim.step()
# (optional) bake poses to kinematic for a fixed-pile eval set
""")

# 5. Post-settle sanity (contact pair explosion is a real risk with 300 rigid bodies)
check_physics_health()

# Realistic expectations to set with Kenji:
#  - cycle time 3-8 s/pick typical for 6-DOF vision-guided bin pick
#  - casting appearance variance, specular surfaces, bin-bottom occlusion = hard parts
#  - MVP: one casting type, controlled lighting, no clutter -- before full pile
#  - ISO 9283 geometric repeatability validatable in sim; perception GT available
failure_modes: Telling Kenji to train his own pose CNN — ignores shipped FoundationPose / IM blocks
Hand-authored pile — not representative of real physics settling for bin picks
Ignoring contact-pair explosion at 300 rigid bodies — PhysX perf collapses at settle time
Quoting a made-up cycle time — 3-8 s/pick is the literature number, cite it
